UNESCO World Heritage · Campania, Italy

The Amalfi
Coast

Fifty kilometres of cliffs plunging into the Tyrrhenian Sea. Clifftop villages, giant lemons, colourful ferries and an Italy that never stops surprising.

Start with Positano Choose your itinerary
Discover

Why this place is different

Not just a destination.
An experience that changes the way you see the sea.

The Amalfi Coast is one of the most photographed coastlines in the world, yet it never fails to surprise visitors in person. The scent of lemons, the sound of morning ferries, alleyways too narrow for cars, lunches that last until sunset. This guide won't just tell you where to go - it'll help you understand how to live it.

Explore the Coast

The Enchanted Villages

Explore the pearls of the coastline, each with its own soul and centuries-old traditions.

All destinations
The living postcard

Positano

Famous for its pastel-coloured houses that defy gravity, luxury boutiques and iconic beaches.

IconicFashionSpiaggia Grande
The ancient Maritime Republic

Amalfi

The historical heart of the coast, dominated by the majestic Cathedral of Sant'Andrea and ancient paper mills.

HistoryCathedralPiazza
The city of music

Ravello

Suspended between sky and sea, offering the most spectacular panoramas from its historic villas and enchanted gardens.

MusicVilla CimbroneViews
The village of sunsets

Praiano

An oasis of tranquillity with breathtaking views and a vibrant local art scene between sea and mountains.

SunsetsRelaxMarina di Praia
The longest beach

Maiori

The most extensive seafront on the Coast, authentic and less crowded, with the remains of a Roman villa.

BeachRelaxAuthenticity
The gastronomic soul

Minori

Famous for limoncello, fresh pasta and the remains of a Roman villa overlooking the sea.

GastronomyRoman VillaLemons

Crystal clear waters

The Most Beautiful Beaches

Spiaggia Grande

Positano

Spiaggia di Ieranto

Massa Lubrense

Marina Piccola

Capri

Fiordo di Furore

Praiano

Read

Did you know

50 km

of coastline

From Positano to Vietri sul Mare, declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1997.

9th c.

birth of Amalfi

First maritime power in Europe, with trade routes reaching Constantinople.

350 m

the altitude of Ravello

Wagner wrote Parsifal here. Gore Vidal lived in Ravello for thirty years.

Start here

Positano.
The living postcard.

No place on the coast is more photographed than Positano, yet no photo truly captures its essence. The pastel-coloured houses, Spiaggia Grande, handmade sandal shops, the scent of lemon everywhere. The complete guide tells you how to get there, where to park (if you insist), when ferries depart and where to eat without breaking the bank.

ZTL active Ferries from Naples Path of the Gods
Complete guide to Positano

Fast Facts · Positano

Altitude

0–350 m

Population

≈ 3,900

ZTL

Yes (summer)

Access

Ferry · Bus

Province

Salerno (SA)

Best season

May · Sep

Amalfi Coast Travel helps travelers from around the world plan their trip to the Amalfi Coast through guides, travel tips, and much more. The entire project is lovingly curated by Alessia, directly from Massa Lubrense.

The Amalfi Coast - A UNESCO World Heritage Site
